The pack is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the RP08 email/DMS pattern (CP272–CP279). Key confirmations:
- 40-unit coverage reconciles across registry, plan, manifest, contract, and baseline
- All no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flags hold; security guards effective per-section
- Authority boundary intact (Claude not final approver; no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation claim)
- Contract deep-equal-bound to code-generated descriptor/requirements/attestation (drift-proof)
- CP178+ hardening satisfied (auth preflight valid, read-only tools, summary-only correctly prohibited)
- `production_ready:false` / `implemented_pending_review` — no overreach; final closeout validation correctly deferred to post-review

Coverage reconciles to 40 across deliverable/phase/micro-phase/micro-title distributions and matches the registry requirements, closeout-pack-plan pack, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and review baseline. The implementation (service.js createEmailDmsCoreCp280GoldenHermesSlice* family) produces descriptor-only case sets with all email/office-native/sync/filing dispatch, DB/object-storage writes, lock acquisition, idempotency/workflow persistence, state-transition writes, rollback/retry runtime, command runtime, and Hermes runtime-receipt flags set false, and synthetic_only/no_real_data/customer_safe_errors_only true. validators.js enforces descriptor-only rows plus per-section security guards (no cross-tenant access, deny-over-allow, no audit-hint/validation-error/blocked-claim/lock-token/permission-decision leakage) wherever the relevant row exists; required rows are structurally guaranteed present so guards are effective. The authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and the no-write attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. Hermes control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/evidence/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The contract validator deep-equals the embedded golden_hermes_slice_descriptor, cp280_requirements, and cp280_no_write_attestation against the code-generated objects, providing strong drift protection; historical bindings tail and current_pack are CP00-280. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (exit 0), summary-only review correctly prohibited (Risk B with security/audit and review-evidence deliverables), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, and the forbidden_review_evidence list (auth failures, zero-byte/malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) is complete. The pack does not assert production_ready=true: command-evidence and manifest report production_ready=false / status implemented_pending_review; the descriptor-verified flag and the Hermes production_ready_candidate (gated on coverage.valid AND slice.valid) are appropriately scoped and not an enterprise-trust overreach. All pre-review validation commands are recorded as exit 0, with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred to the post-review final-closeout step per the hardened sequence. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the eight pack files and excludes unrelated dirty files (LDIP result JSON, the v2.0 .docx, and the handoff note). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; this is a clean continuation of the established CP272-CP279 descriptor-only pattern. Note that Claude is a read-only reviewer and not the final approver — human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim and for opening runtime in downstream packs.","findings":[],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":0,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":0}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"3a884cd7-f886-442f-85b7-7db6983683e9"}
